data.title: OneSpaWorld Q1 revenue rises 13%, beats estimates on cruise ship expansion
data.description:
Overview

  • Global cruise wellness provider's Q1 revenue rose 13% yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS and adjusted EBITDA for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company declared quarterly dividend of $0.05 per share

Outlook

  • OneSpaWorld sees Q2 2026 revenue of $257 mln to $262 mln

  • Company expects Q2 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $32.5 mln to $34.5 mln

  • OneSpaWorld forecasts FY 2026 revenue of $1.014 bln to $1.034 bln

Result Drivers

  • CRUISE SHIP EXPANSION - Revenue growth was driven by a 4% increase in revenue days and health and wellness center expansion from new ship builds

  • HIGHER GUEST SPEND - Average guest spend rose 2%, contributing to increased revenues

  • AI TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION - Co said it is accelerating integration of AI-driven technologies to drive incremental revenue and earnings growth

data.title: Moldova trims GDP growth forecast to 2.2% in 2026
data.description:
Moldova's economy ministry on Wednesday cut its forecast for GDP growth in 2026 to 2.2% from the 2.4% it had expected in November, saying the worsening situation in the Middle East would hit the business environment.

The ministry raised its forecast for 2027 to 3.6% from 3.3%, and for 2028 to 4% from 3.6%.

data.title: Evercore Q1 revenue doubles, beats estimates, raises dividend
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. investment bank's Q1 revenue more than doubled, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company raised quarterly dividend 6% and repurchased 1.9 mln shares

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific financial guidance or outlook for the current or future periods

Result Drivers

  • ADVISORY FEES - Significant increase in advisory fees from large transactions and a higher number of client engagements drove revenue growth

  • TRADING COMMISSIONS - Higher trading commissions from increased trading volume contributed to revenue growth

  • ASSET MANAGEMENT FEES - Asset management and administration fees rose due to higher assets under management and net inflows

data.title: ZAWYA: Majority of auto dealers turn to data and digitisation to strengthen operational, sales and service offerings
data.description:
Dubai, UAE, A huge majority of automotive dealers in the Middle East are now focusing on adapting data and digitisation to reinforce operational, sales and service offering efficiencies and to stay ahead of an increasingly competitive automotive retail market.

According to Keyloop, a leading provider of automotive retail solutions, growing vehicle sales volume targets and rising customer expectations are putting pressure on traditional automotive retail models. For this reason, 94% of automotive dealers in the region are now turning to data and digitisation for solutions.

“Fragmented systems, manual processes and limited data visibility are affecting dealer performance in the region. As sales volumes, brand portfolios and customer touchpoints increase, dealers across the region are facing growing pressure to modernise internal operations to maintain efficiency and service standards,” said Monzer Tohme, Managing Director, MEA & APAC, Keyloop. “Despite dealer operations becoming more complex, many organisations are still relying on outdated systems that limit visibility and hinder decision‑making.”

Tohme added that these dealerships operate across multiple disconnected platforms spanning sales, finance and aftersales, with manual processes and data silos that hinder efficiency. These challenges have a measurable business impact, with industry data showing that dealers lose up to 37 percent of online leads due to missed or delayed follow‑up, while only 1 percent of consumers describe the car‑buying experience as ideal. Additionally, 45 percent of customers are willing to switch car brands due to poor aftersales service. As such, addressing operational inefficiencies has become a priority for maintaining performance and customer confidence in an increasingly competitive market.

The UAE and Saudi Arabia registered sales growth rates of 19.1 percent and 6.6 percent respectively in 2024, making them among the fastest growing automotive markets in the world. That year, the two markets also sold a combined 1.16 million new vehicles and are worth over USD 80 billion. This growth is supported by rising consumer demand and long‑term government investment in mobility and electrification.

"There is a growing consensus among dealers that improved use of data and digital tools is crucial for strengthening operational efficiency,” Tohme explained. “As such, there is a need for clearer, real‑time visibility across sales, finance and aftersales workflows, as well as more consistent use of customer and vehicle data to support faster decisions and reduce manual intervention.”

Keyloop has noted that many dealers across the region are reassessing how data flows through their businesses as volumes rise, brand portfolios expand and regulatory requirements become more demanding.

Furthermore, there are several wider industry dynamics that are contributing to the operational pressures facing automotive retailers. These include rapid entry of new brands and business models, accelerating penetration of electric vehicles, with the UAE leading the GCC at 6 percent, and increasing policy and regulatory expectations around data governance, reporting and customer transparency. These reinforce the need for more resilient and adaptable internal processes for dealers as the regional automotive landscape continues to evolve.

“What we’re seeing across the region is a clear shift in priorities, as dealers recognise that improving operational efficiency is no longer about incremental gains, but about building the foundations needed to operate at scale,” Tohme said. “In a rapidly evolving industry, the ability to access and act on reliable data across the entire vehicle ownership cycle has become central to the business performance of dealers.”

As the region experiences strong sales growth, rapid electrification and changing government policies, effective use of data and digitisation will play an increasingly important role in how dealers can meet the market's needs, he concluded.

Contacts:

For further information, please contact:

Albert Alba

Senior Communications Manager- Aurora the Agency

albert@auroratheagency.com

About Keyloop:

Keyloop is a leading provider of automotive retail solutions, with more than 40 years of industry expertise and over 14 billion digital interactions captured to date. Its cutting-edge solutions cater for every stage of the automotive retail ecosystem, connecting retailers, OEMs, financiers, and fleet suppliers with their consumers via innovative Experience-First software.

Spanning demand, supply, operations and ownership, Fusion is Keyloop’s dynamic Automotive Retail Platform, optimising the automotive retail lifecycle for customers and consumers alike. It increases both vehicle and customer lifetime value through seamless automation and personalisation, while its technology serves more than 20,000 retailers, more than 80 OEMs, 80% of the top 10 UK leasing companies, and 60% of brands within Europe’s financier space. For more information, please visit: www.keyloop.com.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Blackstone Mortgage Trust 10-Q: $159.4M Revenue, $(0.04) EPS
data.description:
Blackstone Mortgage Trust reported total net revenue of $159.4 million for the quarter and a net loss of $6.34 million, or $(0.04) per share, driven by elevated reserves and owned real estate costs. The company noted a modest revenue increase versus the prior quarter while operating results were pressured by higher CECL reserves and owned real estate expenses. These factors produced an operating shortfall and a reported loss for the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenue: $159.4 million
  • Gross Profit: $48.6 million
  • Total expenses: $110.8 million
  • Operating income (loss): $(5.182) million (loss before income taxes)
  • Net loss: $(6.34) million
  • Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(0.04) (weighted-average shares 169.08 million)

Business Highlights

  • Loan origination activity: Originated or acquired $275 million in loans during the quarter and funded $296 million, reflecting continued lending activity amid portfolio repayments.
  • Portfolio composition & performance: Portfolio remains concentrated in senior loans across major markets with 98% of exposure performing and a weighted risk score of 3.0.
  • Owned real estate expansion: Consolidated 13 owned properties, primarily office and hospitality, with a carrying value of $1.3 billion to manage workout outcomes.
  • Joint ventures and strategic deployments: Active deployments in joint ventures including Bank Loan and Net Lease JV, plus participation in a $66.7 million SRT and a $75 million fund commitment to diversify credit exposure.
  • Asset management and credit monitoring: Increased CECL reserves and targeted asset-specific reserves; proactive management actions include foreclosures and workouts to preserve asset value.

Original SEC Filing: BLACKSTONE MORTGAGE TRUST, INC. [ BXMT ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Biogen 10-Q: Q1 revenue $2,477.8M, diluted EPS $2.15
data.description:
Biogen reported first-quarter total revenue of $2,477.8 million, up 1.9% year-over-year, and diluted earnings per share rose to $2.15, an increase of $0.51 (31.1%). Net income was $319.5 million on income before tax of $377.7 million. The quarter reflects modest product revenue growth alongside increased R&D spending tied to pipeline programs and inventory adjustments.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $2,477.8 million, increased $46.8 million or 1.9% vs prior year (Q1 2026)
  • Product revenue, net: $1,752.3 million, increased $25.8 million or 1.5% (Q1 2026)
  • Cost of sales, excluding amortization and impairment of acquired intangible assets: $661.0 million, increased $31.7 million or 5.0% (Q1 2026)
  • Total cost and expense: $2,100.1 million, decreased $19.7 million or (0.9%) (Q1 2026)
  • Income before income tax (benefit) expense: $377.7 million
  • Net income: $319.5 million
  • Diluted earnings per share: $2.15, increased $0.51 (31.1%)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue momentum: Growth driven by anti-CD20 programs (up 10.8%) and the Alzheimer's collaboration (up 80.3%).
  • Product mix and channel dynamics: Multiple sclerosis portfolio shows modest growth overall; TECFIDERA declined due to generic competition while TYSABRI and VUMERITY expanded through pricing and shifts in distribution channels.
  • Brand launches and rare disease growth: Commercial expansion from SKYCLARYS and QALSODY; U.S. launch of ZURZUVAE gained traction with $55.4 million in Q1 sales.
  • Operational capacity: Large-scale Solothurn biologics facility is operational and approved to produce LEQEMBI and TYSABRI to support mid-term manufacturing needs.
  • R&D and pipeline activity: Research and development spending increased 24%, driven by SKYCLARYS inventory step-up and clinical activity; ongoing trials include felzartamab and litifilimab, with positive Phase 2 litifilimab data in CLE reported.

Original SEC Filing: BIOGEN INC. [ BIIB ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
